    1. before(MethodContext)
    2. after(Object, MethodContext)

Interface Interceptor

All Known Implementing Classes:
HeaderInterceptor, PathQueryInterceptor
public interface Interceptor
Contract for all interceptors in the request pipeline.

An interceptor is invoked twice per request:

  • before(MethodContext) – called in registration order before the HTTP call is made. Implementations may enrich the MethodContext (e.g. resolve path variables, inject headers).
  • after(Object, MethodContext) – called in registration order after the response has been deserialized. Implementations may transform or inspect the result.

Interceptors are composed by InterceptorHierarchy.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Object
    after(Object response, MethodContext ctx)
    Invoked after the HTTP response has been deserialized.
    MethodContext
    before(MethodContext ctx)
    Invoked before the HTTP request is dispatched.

  • Method Details

    • before

      MethodContext before(MethodContext ctx)
      Invoked before the HTTP request is dispatched.
      Parameters:
      ctx - the mutable request context; implementations should modify and return it
      Returns:
      the (potentially modified) context that will be passed to the next interceptor

    • after

      Object after(Object response, MethodContext ctx)
      Invoked after the HTTP response has been deserialized.
      Parameters:
      response - the deserialized response object produced by the executor
      ctx - the request context as it was when the call was made
      Returns:
      the (potentially transformed) response that will be passed to the next interceptor
